title: ALL CAPS: THE HISTORY OF THE AMERICAN BASEBALL HAT
chaptersLine: Forty-plus chapters drafted, plus an introduction and a closing coda.
spanLine: From before the Civil War to the drops of 2026.
thisWeek: Sat down with the second founder of a custom-cap studio, who reached the book's central idea from the sales side, the opposite direction his partner came from.
A legendary throwback maker started sending factory-level corrections directly and unprompted, by text and email.
Locked the sourcing on why two clubs spell an everyday word with an X, down to the 1921 language critic who called it a vigorous newcomer.
chapterHighlights: The 1996 World Series phone call that put a red Yankees cap on a filmmaker and quietly changed the rules.
The Brooklyn crew who claimed a luxury label as their own and rewrote who a logo belongs to.
A farmer sitting on a hundred thousand free caps, and the rural economy nobody writes about.
The small patch on the side of a fitted that turned an ordinary cap into a grail.
